title: Liquid-liquid extraction of 3,3'-dichlorobiphenyl (PCB11) from animal blood/serum
author: Xueshu Li
materials: Cap tube-A and the Reference standard .
Invert tube-B for 5 min and centrifuge.
Invert tube-A on the tube rotator and centrifuge.

steps.title: Place whole blood (0
steps.instruction: Place whole blood (0.3-0.5 g) or serum (~ 0.2 g) in tube-A . Record weight on the working sheet.

steps.title: Spike all tube-A samples (MB, TB,...
steps.instruction: Spike all tube-A samples (MB, TB, tissue, OPR, and Reference standard ) with SS_PCB (10 ng d-PCB65 and 10 ng PCB14). d-PCB65, 100 µL x 100 ng/mL = 10 ng each PCB14, 100 µL x 100 ng/mL = 10 ng each

steps.title: Add 1 mL of 6 M...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of 6 M HCl to tube-A , vortex for 10 s.

steps.title: Get samples, ice box, and serological...
steps.instruction: Get samples, ice box, and serological pipettes ready in a fume hood.

steps.title: Add 3 mL of hexane with...
steps.instruction: Add 3 mL of hexane with a repeat pipettor and vortex.

steps.title: Prepare the SPE cartridges. Note
steps.instruction: Prepare the SPE cartridges. Note : The SPE cartridge can also be prepared using the dry reagent loading method: Glass wool is placed on the bottom of the SPE cartridges, and 0.2 g of silica gel and 2 g of acidified silica gel are added. Then, 3 mL of hexane:DCM (1:1) mixture is passed through the cartridge to rinse the filled cartridge. Note : DCM is toxic, need to work in fume hood with good ventilation condition.

steps.title: Spike 100 µL of IS (PCB30+PCB204,...
steps.instruction: Spike 100 µL of IS (PCB30+PCB204, 100 µL x 100 ng/mL = 10 ng each) to each tube-E and Reference standard using a single channel pipette.

steps.title: Spike all tube-A samples (MB, TB,...
steps.instruction: Spike all tube-A samples (MB, TB, tissue, OPR, and reference) with SS_OH-PCB (10 ng 4’-OH-PCB159). 4’-OH-PCB159, 100 µL x 100 ng/mL = 10 ng each

steps.title: Spike the following analytes only to...
steps.instruction: Spike the following analytes only to tube-A OPR samples and the Reference standard . 100 µL of PCB11 standard (100 µL x 100 ng/mL = 10 ng) 100 µL of OH-PCB11 standard mixture (100 µL x 100 ng/mL = 10 ng each)

steps.title: Transfer the organic phase (top layer)...
steps.instruction: Transfer the organic phase (top layer) from tube-A to tube-B with a short glass pipette.

steps.title: Transfer the organic layer (top layer) form tube-B to tube-C
steps.instruction: Transfer the organic layer (top layer) form tube-B to tube-C .

steps.title: Add ~0
steps.instruction: Add ~0.5 mL of diazomethane to each sample and 1 mL of diazomethane to the Reference standard using a 10 mL serological pipette.

steps.title: Cap the tubes and keep them...
steps.instruction: Cap the tubes and keep them in a solvent-proof refrigerator ( NOT freezer ) at 4-8 °C for at least 3 h or overnight (approximately 16 h).

steps.title: Evaporate the Reference standard to near...
steps.instruction: Evaporate the Reference standard to near dryness (~ 50 μ L) and put it aside for the IS spiking step.

steps.title: Evaporate the excess of ether and...
steps.instruction: Evaporate the excess of ether and diazomethane in a fume hood under a gentle nitrogen flow (no yellow color, ~200 µL).

steps.title: Add 2 mL of KOH-95% EtOH...
steps.instruction: Add 2 mL of KOH-95% EtOH solution with a repeat pipettor and vortex.

steps.title: Heat for 1 hour in 50...
steps.instruction: Heat for 1 hour in 50 °C water bath, vortexing the samples approximately every 15 min.

steps.title: Add 7 mL of Mili-Q water...
steps.instruction: Add 7 mL of Mili-Q water with a repeat pipettor, invert, and centrifuge.

steps.title: Rinse tube-E with hexane (~150 µL)...
steps.instruction: Rinse tube-E with hexane (~150 µL) and combine with to the GC vial insert to give a final volume of ~300 µL.

steps.title: With long glass pipettes, slowly load...
steps.instruction: With long glass pipettes, slowly load the extracts from tube-D into the SPE cartridges and pass them through the cartridges for collection. Notes : Adjust the vacuum of the manifold accordingly, and let the eluent drip drop by drop. Close off the vacuum once the gel is dry to avoid evaporating the sample liquid. Always keep the solvent level above silica gel.

steps.title: Replace the waste tubes at the...
steps.instruction: Replace the waste tubes at the bottom with freshly labeled, combusted tube-E . Align tube-D order in the tube rack with the order of tube-E .

steps.title: Quality control samples should include
steps.instruction: Quality control samples should include: Method blank (MB): x2 or x3 to make sample number even Tissue blank (TB): blood or serum from control animals Tissue samples: blood or serum from exposed animals Ongoing Precision and Recovery (OPR): tissue blank spiked with surrogate standard (SS), internal standard (IS), and analytes Reference standards: includes SS, IS, and analytes Instrument blank or solvent: hexane only Note: For the spike test, use only blank blood/serum. For actual tests, use blank blood/serum and exposed samples.

steps.title: Combust silica gel, glass wool, all...
steps.instruction: Combust silica gel, glass wool, all glassware (cartridges, short and long pipettes, tubes, beaker/volumetric flask, funnel), scalpel, forceps, and spatula.

steps.title: Print the working sheet (see attachment for an example)
steps.instruction: Print the working sheet (see attachment for an example).

steps.title: Label tubes as A1, A2 etc, and Reference standard
steps.instruction: Label tubes as A1, A2 etc, and Reference standard .

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note : For the entire method, all centrifugation steps are for 5 mins at 3000 rpm. Tubes are inverted on the tube rotator for 5 mins at 40 RPM. Note : For the evaporation or “blow down” step with nitrogen, a warm water bath (35 °C) can be used if needed.

steps.title: Transfer the organic phase (top layer)...
steps.instruction: Transfer the organic phase (top layer) from tube-A to tube-B with a short pipette.

steps.title: Evaporate the solvent under a gentle...
steps.instruction: Evaporate the solvent under a gentle stream of nitrogen to ~0.5 mL.

steps.title: Rinse tube-D twice (2 x 0
steps.instruction: Rinse tube-D twice (2 x 0.5 mL) with hexane:DCM (1:1) and pass through the cartridge each time.

steps.title: Repeatedly add the hexane:DCM (1:1) mixture...
steps.instruction: Repeatedly add the hexane:DCM (1:1) mixture (total volume ~ 9 mL) until the eluent in tube-E reaches 10 mL.

steps.title: Add 3 mL of 1% KCl...
steps.instruction: Add 3 mL of 1% KCl solution to tube A containing blank samples.

steps.title: Add 5 mL of 2-propanol to...
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL of 2-propanol to tube-A , vortex for 10 s.

steps.title: Add 5 mL of hexane-MTBE mixture 1:1 (v/v) to tube-A
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL of hexane-MTBE mixture 1:1 (v/v) to tube-A .

steps.title: Cap the tube-A with caps with a PTFE septum
steps.instruction: Cap the tube-A with caps with a PTFE septum.

steps.title: Add 3 mL of 1% KCl solution to tube-B
steps.instruction: Add 3 mL of 1% KCl solution to tube-B .

steps.title: Transfer the organic layer (top layer) form tube-B to tube-C
steps.instruction: Transfer the organic layer (top layer) form tube-B to tube-C .

steps.title: Re-extract tube-B with 3 mL of hexane, vortex, and centrifuge
steps.instruction: Re-extract tube-B with 3 mL of hexane, vortex, and centrifuge.

steps.title: Evaporate solvent in tube-C under a...
steps.instruction: Evaporate solvent in tube-C under a gentle stream of nitrogen to ~100 µL. Note: Do not evaporate to dryness.

steps.title: Prepare the solvent blank by filling...
steps.instruction: Prepare the solvent blank by filling a GC vial with hexane. Note : Use the same hexane used for the extractions.

steps.title: Recap the samples after the GC-MS/MS or GC-ECD analysis
steps.instruction: Recap the samples after the GC-MS/MS or GC-ECD analysis.

steps.title: Label all sample tubes ( Tube-A...
steps.instruction: Label all sample tubes ( Tube-A , Tube-B , Tube-C , Tube-D , and Tube-E ).

steps.title: Put the Reference standard aside for the derivatization step
steps.instruction: Put the Reference standard aside for the derivatization step.

steps.title: Re-extract tube-A with 3 mL of...
steps.instruction: Re-extract tube-A with 3 mL of hexane-MTBE (1:1, v/v), vortex for 10 s, and centrifuge.

steps.title: Remove the analytical standards for the...
steps.instruction: Remove the analytical standards for the ORP and the SS from the freezer and allow to warm to room temperature (~ 30 min).

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note : If the whole blood is used for the study, the whole blood samples need to be collected with a medium tube with 80 µL of ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid solution (EDTA, 7.5% w/w), and stored at −20 °C until extraction.

steps.title: Label GC vials
steps.instruction: Label GC vials.

steps.title: Turn on the water bath with setting of 50 °C
steps.instruction: Turn on the water bath with setting of 50 °C.

steps.title: Remove the IS standards from the...
steps.instruction: Remove the IS standards from the freezer and allow to warm to room temperature (~ 30 min).

steps.title: Make fresh KOH-95% EtOH solution and...
steps.instruction: Make fresh KOH-95% EtOH solution and set it aside for the base cleanup step.

steps.title: Let samples sit on the lab...
steps.instruction: Let samples sit on the lab bench for 15 mins, centrifuge, and transfer the top layer to combine with tube-D (volume ~5 mL).

steps.title: Add 2 mL of hexane using...
steps.instruction: Add 2 mL of hexane using a repeat pipettor to tube-C and vortex.

steps.title: Transfer the top layer to tube-D with a short pipette
steps.instruction: Transfer the top layer to tube-D with a short pipette.

steps.title: In a small beaker, mix hexane:DCM...
steps.instruction: In a small beaker, mix hexane:DCM (1:1) with acidified silica gel (5:1), swirl, and add the entire suspension with a short pipette on top of the silica gel in each SPE cartridge.

steps.title: Add uncombusted tubes to the bottom...
steps.instruction: Add uncombusted tubes to the bottom of the SPE manifold to collect the waste eluent from the SPE cartridges.

steps.title: Add 2 mL of hexane:DCM (1:1)...
steps.instruction: Add 2 mL of hexane:DCM (1:1) with a long pipette to rinse to the SPE cartridge.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.2 g of silica gel with a combusted small glass funnel to the SPE cartridges.

steps.title: Add glass wool to the SPE...
steps.instruction: Add glass wool to the SPE cartridge and pack it down with the end of a glass pipette.

steps.title: Replace the top and place the...
steps.instruction: Replace the top and place the clean SPE cartridges into each port.

steps.title: Mix the suspension with a short...
steps.instruction: Mix the suspension with a short glass pipette tip to eliminate air bubbles.

steps.title: Let the silica gel precipitate to...
steps.instruction: Let the silica gel precipitate to the bottom before passing ~ 3 mL of hexane:DCM (1:1) into the bottom waste tube. Note : Always keep the solvent above the acidified silica gel.

steps.title: Add 3 mL of hexane to...
steps.instruction: Add 3 mL of hexane to tube-E , then concentrate the sample under a gentle stream of nitrogen to ~50 µL. Note : Do not evaporate to dryness.

steps.title: Concentrate the samples in tube-E under...
steps.instruction: Concentrate the samples in tube-E under a gentle stream of nitrogen to ~200 µL.

steps.title: Turn off the manifold vacuum and...
steps.instruction: Turn off the manifold vacuum and remove tube-E from the SPE manifold.

steps.title: Transfer extracts from tube-E and the...
steps.instruction: Transfer extracts from tube-E and the Reference standard with a long pipette to a combusted crimp-style GC vial with insert.

steps.title: Vortex each tube-E
steps.instruction: Vortex each tube-E .

steps.title: Dispose of all items as follows
steps.instruction: Dispose of all items as follows: SPE Cartridges—Remove the cartridges from the SPE manifold and dispose of all content in the PCB waste container. Place the cartridges in the sink to be washed. Add the original waste tubes to the manifold, open the vacuum tube on the manifold, and turn on the vacuum. Clean the cartridge with acetone and then hexane. Turn off the vacuum and remove it. Let the top dry on its end, and once fully dry, place the manifold back on the shelf. Evaporator – Place needles in the beaker to be combusted. Tube-A , Tube-B , Tube-C , Tube-D , Tube-E , and other aqueous solutions – Aqueous hazardous waste Organic solvents (waste tubes with hexane and 2-propanol) – Organic hazardous waste All PCB-exposed disposable glassware, foil, GC vials, and gloves – Blue PCB hazardous waste container. Serological pipette – glass hazard container.

steps.title: Store samples in solvent-proof -20°C freezer until instrument analysis
steps.instruction: Store samples in solvent-proof -20°C freezer until instrument analysis. Note : Do not store samples in a regular -20°C freezer because solvent vapors will destroy the freezer over time.

steps.title: Cap all vials immediately with a...
steps.instruction: Cap all vials immediately with a crimp cap and crimper to seal the cap.

steps.title: Store all samples, including the solvent...
steps.instruction: Store all samples, including the solvent blank, in a solvent-proof -20°C freezer until the data are published. Note : Do not store samples in a regular -20°C freezer because solvent vapors will destroy the freezer over time.

steps.title: Take the diazomethane vial out of...
steps.instruction: Take the diazomethane vial out of freezer and place in an ice box in the fume hood.

steps.title: Add 1% KCl solution to the...
steps.instruction: Add 1% KCl solution to the remaining tube A to a final volume of ~ 3 mL. Note: Do not add 1% KCl solution to the tube for the Reference standard .

steps.title: Add 5 drops of methanol to...
steps.instruction: Add 5 drops of methanol to tube-C , except for the Reference standard , and vortex for 5 s.
